runs.explanation: Lines 86-87 incorrectly remove the last element when both streams share the same final value, but the docstring states duplicates should be preserved from both sources. This post-processing step contradicts the intended merge behavior and breaks correctness for cases where both streams end with equal values.
runs.raw:
Looking at the code, lines 86-87 contain suspicious logic that removes the last element when both streams end with the same value. This is incorrect behavior - if both streams contain the same final value, both copies should be preserved (as stated in the docstring: "Duplicate values that appear in both streams are preserved (one copy from each source)"). This post-processing step incorrectly removes one of the duplicate entries.
